case_title: HERMANPREET SINGH VS. MERIDIAN BUILDERS & DEVELOPERS, INC. et al

docket_entries.description: LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT HOMEAWAY, INC.'S MOTION TO DISMISS AND TRANSFER VENUE IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HOMEAWAY'S TERMS AND CONDITIONS WERE AGREED TO BY THE INDIVIDUAL (REFERRED TO AS "YOU" IN THE TERMS AND CONDITIONS) WHO RENTED THE HOUSE, NOT PLAINTIFF. THE TERMS AND CONDITONS INCLUDED A FORUM SELECTION CLAUSE THAT PROVIDES TRAVIS COUNTY, TEXAS IS THE EXCLUSIVE FORUM FOR "ANY CAUSE OF ACTION YOU MAY SUBMIT IN CONNECTION WITH YOUR USE OF THE SITE OR PURSUANT TO THESE TERMS." PLAINTFF, HOWEVER, WAS NOT A SIGNATORY TO THE CONTRACT. (DECLARATION OF COURTNEY DICKEY, EX. A.) PLAINTIFF IS NOT SO "CLOSELY RELATED TO THE CONTRACTUAL RELATIONSHIP" THAT THE FORUM SELECTION CLAUSE SHOULD APPLY. (SEE NET2PHONE, INC. V. SUP. CT. (2003) 109 CAL.APP.4TH 583, 587.) THE CASES RELIED ON BY DEFENDANT ARE DISTINGUISHABLE. SEE, E.G., NET2PHONE, SUPRA, 109 CAL. APP. 4TH AT 589 ( NET2PHONE SUING IN A REPRESENTATIVE CAPACITY AND PURPORTING TO ASSERT RIGHTS OF THE PARTIES TO THE CONTRACT; IT "STOOD IN THE SHOES OF THOSE WHOM IT PURPORTS TO REPRESENT" AND WAS BOUND BY THE FORUM SELECTION CLAUSE); MANETTI-FARROW, INC. V. GUCCI AMERICA, INC. 858 F.2D 509, 514 N.5 (9TH CIR. 1988) (NON-SIGNATORY GUCCI DEFENDANTS COULD ENFORCE FORUM SELECTION CLAUSE WHERE CONDUCT OF ALL GUCCI DEFENDANTS (INCLUDING SIGNATORY GUCCI PARFUMS) ENGAGED IN CONDUCT THAT WAS CLOSELY RELATED TO CONTRACTUAL RELATIONSHIP); LU V. DRYCLEAN-USA OF CALIFORNIA, INC. (1992) 11 CAL. APP. 4TH 1490, 1494) (NON-SIGNATORY DEFENDANTS ALLEGED TO BE ALTER EGOS OF SIGNATORY DEFENDANT COULD ENFORCE FORUM SELECTION CLAUSE; "TO HOLD OTHERWISE WOULD BE TO PERMIT A PLAINTIFF TO SIDESTEP A VALID FORUM SELECTION CLAUSE SIMPLY BY NAMING A CLOSELY RELATED PARTY WHO DID NOT SIGN THE CLAUSE AS A DEFENDANT"). ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR# 5976.(302/MJM)

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED IN PART AND SUSTAINED IN PART. AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PREMISES LIABILITY, THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR STRICT PRODUCT LIABILITY, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF WARRANTY, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. (SEE MINI-MINUTES AND/OR ORDER FOR COMPLETE RULING). ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: PAULA SHI, CSR # 13358; DEPT. 302. =(302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-20-2015 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT HOMEAWAY, INCORPORATED'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is Overruled in part and sustained in part. As to the first cause of action for premises liability, the demurrer is overruled. The complaint adequately alleges the elements of premises liability. (Complaint, pars. 8, 15-16.) As to the second cause of action for strict product liability, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. The complaint does not allege that Defendant manufactured or marketed any "tangible personal property." (Restatement 3d, Torts: Product Liability sec. 19.) Further, Peterson v. Sup. Ct. (1995) 10 Cal.4th 1185, 1190, provides that a cause of action for strict product liability cannot be maintained under these circumstances, as it would be an "unwarranted extension of the doctrine of products liability." As to the third cause of action for breach of warranty, the demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. The complaint does not allege that Plaintiff was in contractual privity with Defendant. "Privity of contract is a prerequisite in California for recovery on a theory of breach of implied warranties of fitness and merchantability." (All West Electronics, Inc. v. M-B-W, Inc. (1998) 64 Cal.App.4th 717, 725.) Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Paula Shi, CSR # 13358 =302/ehg

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-06-2014 Continued To Dec-03-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss And Transfer Venue
Off Calendar Oct-15-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Kirsten McNelly Bibbes, Esq. of Gordon & Rees LLP, T:415.986.5900, for defendant and moving party;
Blair K. Walsh, Esq. of Abramson Smith Waldsmith LLP, T:415.421.7995, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. After argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling. DEFENDANT HOMEAWAY, INC.'s Motion To Dismiss And Transfer Venue is Denied. Defendant Homeaway's Terms and Conditions were agreed to by the individual (referred to as "you" in the Terms and Conditions) who rented the house, not plaintiff. The Terms and Conditons included a forum selection clause that provides Travis County, Texas is the exclusive forum for "any cause of action you may submit in connection with your use of the site or pursuant to these terms." Plaintff, however, was not a signatory to the contract. (Declaration of Courtney Dickey, Ex. A.) Plaintiff is not so "closely related to the contractual relationship" that the forum selection clause should apply. (See Net2Phone, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 583, 587.) The cases relied on by Defendant are distinguishable. See, e.g., Net2Phone, supra, 109 Cal. App. 4th at 589 ( Net2Phone suing in a representative capacity and purporting to assert rights of the parties to the contract; it "stood in the shoes of those whom it purports to represent" and was bound by the forum selection clause); Manetti-Farrow, Inc. v. Gucci America, Inc. 858 F.2d 509, 514 n.5 (9th Cir. 1988) (non-signatory Gucci defendants could enforce forum selection clause where conduct of all Gucci defendants (including signatory Gucci Parfums) engaged in conduct that was closely related to contractual relationship); Lu v. Dryclean-USA of California, Inc. (1992) 11 Cal. App. 4th 1490, 1494) (non-signatory defendants alleged to be alter egos of signatory defendant could enforce forum selection clause; "to hold otherwise would be to permit a plaintiff to sidestep a valid forum selection clause simply by naming a closely related party who did not sign the clause as a defendant"). ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR# 5976.
